stories.title: Ukraine hits Russian tanker in Sea of Azov
stories.summary: Russia said a Ukrainian drone hit a tanker as it was entering the canal between the Sea of Azov and the Black Sea on Sunday, as Kyiv intensifies its attacks on Russian fuel supplies. The governor of Russia’s Rostov region, Yury Slyusar, said on Telegram that a drone strike caused a fire on the vessel but that it had since been contained . The drone attack came after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced Kyiv would set up a new military unit instructed to make incursions deep in

stories.title: Polish PM pledges memorial to victims of WW2 'genocide by Ukrainian nationalists'
stories.summary: Warsaw and Kyiv have for decades been at loggerheads over the killings of many thousands of civilians during World War Two.

stories.title: Évente százmilliárdokat kap Magyarország klímaátállásra, nem mindegy, mire költjük
stories.summary: A jövő energiapolitikájának nem elsősorban az energiaárakat kell támogatnia, hanem az embereket. Ez különösen fontos idehaza, ahol a lakásállomány energiahatékonysága az egyik legrosszabb az unióban.

stories.title: Nem az a kérdés, hogy megtámadnak-e a kiberbűnözők, hanem az, hogy felkészültél-e rá
stories.summary: Magyarországon az online csalók által okozott károk ma még fájóan nagy arányban az áldozatok veszteségei maradnak, a legtöbb esetben nem jár kártérítés. Ez azonban lassan változhat: a szabályozók egyre inkább azt várják el, hogy a védekezés felelősségéből a bankok és más piaci szereplők is nagyobb részt vállaljanak.

stories.title: Meghalt Szolnoki Péter, a Bon-Bon énekese
stories.summary: Súlyos betegségben hunyt el, 56 éves volt. A Bon-Bon zenekar májusban még jublileumi nagykoncertet adott a Budapest Arénéban. Júniusban jelentették be, hogy Szolnoki Péter kórházban van.

stories.title: A bérgyilkos a legjobb társ egy nyaralásra
stories.summary: Heti streamingajánló Anya Taylor-Joy bűnügyi sorozatával, a magyar pornóiparról szóló dokumentumsorozattal és Octavia Spencer vígjátéksorozatával.

stories.title: „Teljesen kimerültem” – Haaland is elismerte, már előbb le kellett volna cserélni az angolok elleni vb-negyeddöntőben
stories.summary: A norvég kapitány szerint 10 perccel tovább volt pályán, mint kellett volna, Haaland még ennél is korábban lecserélte volna magát.

stories.title: 412 millió forint jogdíjat fizetett ki magának Orbán János Dénes egy év alatt
stories.summary: Az általa vezetett Kárpát-medencei Tehetséggondozó Nonprofit Kft. nyilvános beszerzési listáján más írótól vagy költőtől nem szerepel hasonló nagyságrendű jogvásárlás.

stories.title: Lehet, hogy mondta Messi a bírónak, hogy tisztelettel beszéljen vele, de valami nem stimmel a sztorival
stories.summary: Az argentin válogatott csapatkapitánya egy szabadrúgás után tette helyre az argentin–svájci játékvezetőjét. Vagy nem.

stories.title: Mi lesz a magyar bor jövője?
stories.summary: A világ borpiaca zsugorodik, a fogyasztás több fontos piacon csökken. Ebben a környezetben kellene úgy eladni a magyar bort, hogy az ne csak mennyiségben, hanem értékben is előrelépést jelentsen.

stories.title: Was Erdoğan's gun gift a faux pas or an old-school diplomatic tradition?
stories.summary: When NATO leaders opened their gift boxes from the Turkish president and found a .357 Magnum revolver, some panicked and others laughed. But gifting heads of state lavish, personalised firearms is a tradition as old as Samuel Colt — and as recent as a Czech pistol presented to Trump in 2019.

stories.title: Újra sztrájkolt az IKEA több mint 200 dolgozója, Budaörsön egy időre az árukiadó és a vevőszolgálat is leállt
stories.summary: Sorban harmadik napja teszik le a munkát a magyarországi IKEA dolgozói, a cég menedzsereket küld ki sztrájktörőknek.

stories.title: „Elvileg 178, de ezt sokan kétségbe vonják, szóval egyezzünk ki 176-ban” – random kérdésekre válaszolgat Magyar Péter a Redditen
stories.summary: Pörög a kormányfői AMA.

stories.title: A fidelitasos Nyerges Csenge kiposztolta, hogy Toroczkai lesz a következő miniszterelnök, majd hosszan sorolta, miért megy rossz irányba a Fidesz megújulása
stories.summary: Nyerges egy videóját tavaly nyáron még Orbán Viktor is megosztotta.

stories.title: A fenenagy liberalizmusban nem egyszerű kelet-európainak lenni, ha nem egyenesen lehetetlen
stories.summary: A vallásos román családot meghurcolják a norvégok, mert szálltak az atyai jellegű pofonok. Cannes-i nagydíjas film a fenenagy északi liberalizmusról.

stories.title: Letartóztattak egy férfit Ohio államban, de kiengedték, mert az orvosi ellátása „csődbe vihette volna a megyét”
stories.summary: Sidersnek eredetileg 300 ezer dollár óvadékot kellett volna fizetnie, de ettől eltekintettek.

stories.title: Gyógyszeráfa, azbeszt és közvetlen elnökválasztás – ezeket a komolyabb ígéreteket lehet majd számon kérni Magyar Péteren a reddites válaszai alapján
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök válaszaiból többek közt kiderült, hogy dolgoznak a gyógyszerek áfájának csökkentésén, van terv a GVH elnökének leváltására és nem vennék el a határon túliak szavazati jogát.

stories.title: Sándor-palota: Szabályszerűen kifizették Sulyok Márton amerikai útjának költségeit
stories.summary: Magyar Péter szombat délelőtt posztolt a köztársasági elnök és fia amerikai útjáról.

stories.title: Poland could cut arms aid to Ukraine as WWII spat continues
stories.summary: As Poland observes a day of remembrance for the victims of a Ukrainian nationalist military unit, there are increasing signs that it could cut off its neighbor from military aid, even as the war against Russia continues.

stories.title: Newly released footage shows revolver gifted by Erdogan to NATO leaders
stories.summary: Images shared by the Lithuanian Presidency show a revolver given by Turkey's Erdogan to every NATO leader as a parting gift after the summit in Ankara. Each leader received a box containing a revolver engraved with their name, six bullets and a note exempting the weapons from export control.

stories.title: France fans erupt as Les Bleus down Morocco to reach semis
stories.summary: Scenes of jubilation unfolded in Paris and at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, Massachusetts, as Les Bleus beat Morocco 2-0 on 09 July to secure a place in the FIFA World Cup semi-finals.

stories.title: Hungary public media reform begins with black screen apology
stories.summary: People tuning in to public service media in Hungary on Tuesday were greeted by a black screen and an apology. Peter Magyar has begun dismantling one of Viktor Orban's most potent tools of power.

stories.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran following attack on vessel in Hormuz strait
stories.summary: The U.S. launched new strikes on Iran in the early hours on Sunday in retaliation for Tehran’s attack on a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, further jeopardizing an  already fragile ceasefire agreement between the two countries. The U.S. military’s Central Command said it hit roughly 140 military targets during the strikes, including Iranian ​missile and drone sites, naval capabilities and ammunition storage ⁠facilities. The strikes were in response to Iran attacking a ship i

stories.title: Iran attacks Gulf countries following fresh US strikes – Middle East crisis live
stories.summary: Jordan, Bahrain, Qatar, Oman and the UAE reportedly came under attack as Tehran says it is closing Strait of Hormuz There has been almost no visible traffic in the strait of Hormuz so far today, with only two oil products tankers seen approaching the narrow waterway, according to a Bloomberg report . As a reminder, the US president, Donald Trump, has declared the ceasefire over while leaving the door open for talks, and mediators have been trying to salvage a diplomatic solution despite the atta

stories.title: Trump praises Lindsey Graham as ‘one of the greatest people I have ever known’ after senator dies of sudden illness – live
stories.summary: Graham’s abrupt death will send shock waves through Washington and the Republican party Lindsey Graham, key ally of Donald Trump, has died after sudden illness, his office says Sign up for the Breaking News US email The statement issued by Lindsey Graham ’s office said only that the senator had “passed away from a brief and sudden illness”. It gave no further details. Citing police scanner audio, the Washington Post (paywall) reports that emergency medical services had received a call at about 8

stories.title: US: Reporters subpoenaed over Air Force One stories
stories.summary: New York Times journalists who reported on security fears about the president's new Qatari-gifted plane have been called to court. The case raises concerns about press freedoms under the Trump administration.

stories.title: EU defense remains heavily reliant on the United States
stories.summary: There are many obstacles as EU governments seek to reduce their dependence on the US. DW looks at the European Unions's attempts to develop counterparts to Patriot and Tomahawk missiles, F-35 jets, and Starlink internet.

stories.title: Iran launches major missile attacks across the Gulf
stories.summary: Euronews journalists in Qatar report two waves of Iranian attacks and large explosions above Doha, air defence also responding to Iranian attacks in UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait and others, hours after the US conducted new airstrikes in Iran in response to a ship being hit by Iran in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump administration panned for New York Times subpoenas
stories.summary: Press advocacy groups panned the federal government’s move on Friday to subpoena a group of New York Times journalists who reported on security issues plaguing U.S. President Donald Trump’s new Air Force One plane . The Times wrote in a  story published Saturday  that its journalists — which included Tyler Pager, Julian Barnes, Eric Lipton and Eric Schmitt — had been told to testify by a grand jury in Manhattan this coming week. The subpoenas, according to

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy decries housing of weapons in civilian area after Russian strike kills 10
stories.summary: President says tragedy must never be repeated after secondary explosions from strike devastate residential area on Kyiv outskirts. What we know on day 1,600 Continue reading...

stories.title: Victorian crossbench MP warns voting system overhaul must be backed by broader reform – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Victorian Animal Justice party MP Georgie Purcell says abolishing the state’s group voting ticket system without any other changes would see the balance of power handed to One Nation. The Age reported yesterday that the Allan government is planning to introduce legislation, when parliament returns from the winter break, to reform the state’s unusual voting system. Crossbench MPs like myself support electoral re

stories.title: Toronto shooting: two dead and four injured at Salsa on St Clair street festival
stories.summary: Police say two people exchanged gunfire in shooting that mayor called an ‘irresponsible act of violence’ in festival attended by families A shooting near a Toronto street festival killed two men and wounded four other people on Saturday evening, police said, adding that what initially prompted an active-shooter warning was an exchange of gunfire between two people targeting each other. Toronto police deputy chief Frank Barredo said investigators recovered two firearms after the shooting, which w

stories.title: A revolution in ruins: fury amid the rubble of a housing project in quake-hit Venezuela
stories.summary: Discontent with Trump-backed government mounts as Chávez heirs struggle to respond to disaster for which they seem ill-prepared Even before two powerful earthquakes reduced the OPPE 25 government housing project to an anarchy of shattered concrete and broken lives, the foundations of Hugo Chávez’s populist “Bolivarian” revolution were shaking in what was once a hotbed of support. Gabriel González remembers his elation when, in 2013, he received the keys to his freshly completed apartment in one

stories.title: EU accused of dragging its feet over ban on trade with illegal Israeli settlements
stories.summary: Foreign ministers will discuss options on Monday but decision on imports is not expected for months The EU has been accused of dragging its feet over upholding international law, on the eve of a long-awaited debate about banning trade with illegal Israeli settlements. EU foreign ministers meeting in Brussels on Monday will discuss a possible ban on imports from the settlements, against an ongoing humanitarian crisis in Gaza , where a UN inquiry found Israel to be committing a genocide , and surg

stories.title: Missing woman’s former partner charged with murder after remains found in Queensland bushland
stories.summary: Toowoomba woman Jana Armstrong, mother of a four-month-old baby, was last seen on Tuesday Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Queensland police have charged a former partner of a missing woman with murder, after a body believed to be hers was discovered in bushland by a group of hunters. As of Sunday, the remains were yet to be formally identified but police investigators said they believed they were those of Jana Ar

stories.title: Lindsey Graham, key ally of Donald Trump, has died after sudden illness, his office says
stories.summary: Republican served in Senate since 2003 and was sharp Trump critic before becoming one of his most loyal backers Lindsey Graham, a longtime US senator and key ally of Donald Trump, has died from a sudden illness, his office said on Sunday. He had just turned 71. Graham’s abrupt death will send shock waves through Washington and the Republican party. He had served in the Senate since 2003, representing South Carolina, and was running for re-election in November. Continue reading...

stories.title: New anti-racism standard will force Australian universities to use definitions of antisemitism and Islamophobia
stories.summary: Higher education chiefs, academics and students to give evidence this week at royal commission Australian universities will be required to adopt definitions on antisemitism, Islamophobia and racism towards Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people from next year, under a legally enforceable standard designed to stamp out discrimination on campuses. Details of the anti-racism standard will be published on Monday as university bosses, students and academics prepare to appear at the royal commis

stories.title: Elon Musk’s family foundation took Tommy Robinson to Russia, says Musk’s father
stories.summary: Errol Musk says far-right activist is ‘a fine young man’ and held meetings with Russian business figures Elon Musk’s family foundation took Tommy Robinson to Russia, according to the billionaire X owner’s father, who was with the British far-right activist in Moscow as he encouraged anti-migration protests in Britain. Robinson – whose real name is Stephen Yaxley-Lennon – appeared last month in Moscow , from where he issued calls for supporters to take to the streets after a knife attack in Belfa

stories.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
stories.summary: Graham, a prominent Republican voice on foreign policy, had recently returned from Ukraine, where he met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy in Kyiv.

stories.title: US Senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
stories.summary: The influential politician had just returned from Kyiv, where he met Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday.

stories.title: Lindsey Graham dies at 71 after ‘brief and sudden illness,’ his office says
stories.summary: Lindsey Graham, the Republican U.S. senator from South Carolina, died on Saturday after a “brief and sudden illness,” his office said in a statement. “On the evening of Saturday, July 11, U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham passed away from a brief and sudden illness,” the statement said. “Senator Graham’s family appreciates prayers at this time and asks for privacy during this incredibly difficult period.” He was 71. Graham, who first won his office in 2002, had been a fixture in Republi

stories.title: New suspect arrested in Ann Widdecombe murder
stories.summary: Police in southwest England late Saturday said a 28-year-old man has been arrested on suspicion of the murder of Ann Widdecombe. “The suspect, who is a white British national, is now in police custody,” the Devon and Cornwall police said in a statement . The man was arrested in South Yorkshire Saturday evening, the police said . Widdecombe, a former Conservative minister turned Reform UK spokesperson, was found with serious injuries at her home in Haytor, Devon, on Thursday. Widdecom

stories.title: Trump signals support for Patriot production in Ukraine
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says he may let Ukraine produce Patriot air defense missiles. Analysts welcome the move as a sign of support for the country — but warn it could take years before production begins.

stories.title: To make wine is to believe in the future: the Ukrainians growing grapes on the frontline
stories.summary: In the midst of unexploded Russian rockets and buzzing drones, Ukrainian winemakers are keeping vines and hope alive As winemaker Mykhailo Molchanov pottered about trimming foliage from his vines on a warm early-summer day, his dog Direktor at his heels, it was hard to imagine a more idyllic scene. The Molchanovs’ organic vines are planted directly into the richly biodiverse grassland for which southern Ukraine is renowned – hence their label’s name, Steppe Wines – amid the silvery feathergrass

stories.title: First patients enrolled in record-breaking Ebola treatment trial in DRC
stories.summary: Two drugs are being trialled in the Ituri region in a programme set up just six weeks after the outbreak was declared, with hopes it will reduce mortality rates There is no approved drug to help the medical teams scrabbling to save lives in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo – but there are hopes that could change within months as the first patients are enrolled in a treatment trial. It is a record pace to set up and start this kind of research, scientists said, with pati

stories.title: Reeves tells Burnham to expect ‘shocks and challenges’ from outset in No 10
stories.summary: Chancellor says PM-in-waiting needs ‘worked through plan’, in what could be one of her final interviews in No 11 Rachel Reeves has urged Andy Burnham to arrive in Downing Street with a “worked through plan”, saying the incoming prime minister will be tested quickly by a range of incoming “shocks and challenges”. In what could be one of the first female chancellor’s final major interviews while in No 11, Reeves said Burnham should remain focused on the priorities that first brought him into polit

stories.title: Tommy Robinson’s Musk-funded Russia trip spurs call to defend UK democracy
stories.summary: Ed Davey voices concern about the Musk family foundation taking the far-right activist on a visit to Moscow The UK must do more to defend its democracy after it emerged that Elon Musk’s family foundation had taken the far-right activist Tommy Robinson to Russia, Ed Davey has said. Robinson, whose real name is Stephen Yaxley-Lennon, was brought to Russia by the Musks, the billionaire tech mogul’s father told the Guardian . Continue reading...

stories.title: New LS Lowry exhibition aims to demolish ‘naive and uncultured’ myth
stories.summary: Gallery director says collection of 140 paintings will offer a more balanced view of Manchester painter’s work A new exhibition of work by LS Lowry will “bust a few myths” about the Mancunian artist, who the show’s co-curator says is still wrongly derided for being “naive and uncultured”. LS Lowry: the Theatre of Life features 140 paintings by the artist, who captured working-class life in the industrial north-west of England during the early and mid 20th century. Continue reading...

stories.title: Nothing to suggest Ann Widdecombe death politically motivated, say police
stories.summary: Officers say they are not looking for anyone else after arrest of man, 28, on suspicion of murdering ex-Tory politician Police have said there is nothing to suggest the death of Ann Widdecombe was politically motivated. Speaking at a press conference on Sunday morning, the assistant chief constable of Devon and Cornwall police, Matt Longman, said detectives were open-minded about the motive for the killing, but stressed there was no evidence to suggest it had been politically motivated. He also

stories.title: Outcry after Atlanta tosses unhoused people’s belongings near World Cup spot
stories.summary: City official says staffers were performing ‘routine park maintenance’ where 15 people have gathered for months City employees in Atlanta, Georgia, recently threw away tents, medication, identification and other belongings of unhoused people at a public park without warning. This led activists and a local official to point to an apparent violation of procedures created after a city employee ran over a tent with a front loader last year, killing a man. The sweep through the park occurred less tha

stories.title: US and Iran exchange strikes as Tehran declares strait of Hormuz closed again
stories.summary: Tehran launches retaliatory attacks across region after hit on container ship draws US aerial bombardment Middle East crisis live – latest updates Iran has declared the strait of Hormuz closed after six days of hostilities with the US, reversing an agreement signed last month that was intended to restore maritime traffic through the waterway and pave the way for a broader peace deal. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced the closure on Sunday after an intense exchange of aerial

stories.title: France and UK to increase staffing at border controls in effort to stave off travel chaos
stories.summary: Disruption at Channel crossings expected to rise amid new fingerprinting and facial recognition checks France and the UK have agreed to increase staffing at border controls in response to warnings of travel chaos caused by new fingerprinting and facial recognition checks. Disruption at Channel crossings is expected to rise sharply next weekend at the start of the summer holiday season, with MPs warning of “utter chaos and miles of tailbacks” unless the EU’s entry/exit system (EES) is fixed or ch

stories.title: Zohran Mamdani riding high despite New York Post’s daily demonization
stories.summary: The media-savvy mayor’s popularity has only grown as the Murdoch-owned tabloid has thrown everything at him The rightwing New York Post has attacked Zohran Mamdani as a communist, a hater of the police, an antisemite, a driver-away-of-billionaires, and as someone who isn’t very good at bench press. But six months into his mayoralty, Mamdani has so far succeeded where most of his predecessors have failed: he has bested the city’s most powerful tabloid. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘We plant belonging’: how nature charities and asylum seekers work together in UK countryside
stories.summary: Environmental and refugee groups have joined forces to benefit lives and wildlife in Wales and elsewhere Shielding his eyes from the blinding midday sun, Abdullah, a Sudanese asylum seeker, gazes out at the expanse of green in Tŷ Mawr country park in north Wales. “This place is so beautiful,” he says. “It feels a very long way from the Home Office.” Continue reading...

stories.title: US congressman says he was detained by armed Israeli settlers in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: Ro Khanna said settlers were armed with US-made weapons and Israel Defense Forces refused to intervene The US congressman Ro Khanna says armed Israeli settlers detained him during a visit to the Israel -occupied West Bank recently, describing the experience as a first-hand view of the realities faced by Palestinians living under occupation. In an interview with Reuters on Thursday from a Palestinian village, the progressive US House Democrat from California said his detention happened the previo

stories.title: AI companies want to water down Australia’s copyright laws. Artists are outraged, Labor is split
stories.summary: Anthony Albanese will deliver a landmark speech on AI this week as MPs are torn between attracting datacentre investment and protecting the rights of creatives Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates When Anna Funder stood before a pack of journalists at Parliament House this month, she presented herself not just as a writer but also a “victim of crime”. The Stasiland author was using the analogy to illustrate how technology companies have flagrantly “hoovered up” her literary wor

stories.title: Gas queues grow as Ukraine targets Russia's fuel supply
stories.summary: Attacks by Ukraine have triggered a fuel crisis in Russia. Reports from across Russia show how residents are dealing with the disruptions in their daily lives.

stories.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham dies
stories.summary: The South Carolina politician and staunch ally of Donald Trump died aged 71 after ​a brief and sudden illness, his office said. The hawkish senator backed the 2003 invasion of Iraq and the more recent war with Iran.

stories.title: How a Jewish heir is taking on Germany's far-right AfD
stories.summary: Heavily supported in eastern states, the Alternative for Germany is using a GDR cultural icon, the Schwalbe moped, in its campaigning. Now, the Jewish heir of the manufacturer is fighting to protect his family's legacy.

stories.title: Qatar: Former Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies at 74
stories.summary: The former Emir led Qatar's transformation from a small Gulf state into one of the world's wealthiest nations and oversaw the launch of Al Jazeera and the country's World Cup bid.

stories.title: Germany news: Neuschwanstein killer fights extradition to US
stories.summary: A man who murdered a woman at Neuschwanstein Castle says he fears execution in the US. Germany has been hit by another heat wave, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say. DW has more.

stories.title: Germany's Ruhr Valley is on brink of financial collapse
stories.summary: With tax revenues down and social spending up, more and more cities and towns in the Ruhrgebiet are struggling to make ends meet. One city that is particularly deep in the red is Oberhausen.

stories.title: Germany: Left-wing group says it is behind railway sabotage
stories.summary: The "Angry Birds Commando" group said they placed incendiary devices on a railroad between the two busy cities of Cologne and Düsseldorf. The group blamed industrial technology for the destruction of the environment.

stories.title: At least two killed in Toronto street festival shooting
stories.summary: Police say there was an exchange of gunfire between two individuals at the festival, and two firearms had been recovered from the scene.

stories.title: How Aldi is taking on US supermarkets with its $4 almond butter
stories.summary: The German supermarket's $9bn US push targets urban hubs like Manhattan. Can its discount model match Walmart?

stories.title: Former Qatar ruler Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
stories.summary: Sheikh Hamad was known for having helped bring about huge economic, social, and cultural change in Qatar during his reign and helping establish the nation as a global player in diplomacy and investment.

stories.title: US launches fresh strikes as Iran closes Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The strikes follow an attack on a Cyprus -flagged vessel travelling through the critical waterway.

stories.title: People flee as Russian strike hits near Ukraine coffee shop
stories.summary: Three cameras caught the moment people ducked then ran for cover in Sumy, in north eastern Ukraine.

stories.title: Argentina fans erupt as Messi's men reach World Cup semifinal
stories.summary: Argentina fans celebrate in Buenos Aires after a dramatic 3-1 extra-time win over ten-man Switzerland sends the defending champions into the World Cup semifinals.

stories.title: Jude Bellingham double sends jubilant England into World Cup semis
stories.summary: England beat Norway 2-1 in extra time on 12 July 2026 to reach the World Cup semifinals. Fans celebrated in London after a Jude Bellingham double sent the Three Lions through to face Argentina.

stories.title: Badly burned British couple rescued from ravine during Spain wildfires, reports say
stories.summary: The unnamed pair were found semi-conscious with 40% burns after being trapped by the blazes raging through Almeria province, according to local media

stories.title: More than 40 kidnapped children and teachers freed after Nigerian army operation
stories.summary: "I feel happy and elated… I feel joy," the head of the local teachers' union tells the BBC.

stories.title: Trump administration subpoenas New York Times journalists over Air Force One reporting
stories.summary: The reporters received the legal summons after they reported on alleged security issues with the president's new plane, which was gifted by Qatar.

stories.title: Zelenskyy to replace Ukraine’s prime minister
stories.summary: Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Sunday said he intends to replace the country’s ​Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko as part of a major government reshuffle in Kyiv. “Ukraine is changing its political strategy,” Zelenskyy said in a social media post . “For each priority foreign policy direction, a specific person with significant experience will be responsible, capable of implementing what we agree upon at the leader level,” he said. “Personnel ch

stories.title: ‘Politicians have always been schemers’: upheld conviction fails to dent Le Pen’s popularity
stories.summary: Presidential bid by leader of far-right National Rally has no shortage of supporters in scenic Montargis In the small French town of Montargis, Jean-Antoine, a retired decorator, was pleased Marine Le Pen had again shaken up French politics by launching a bid for the presidency , despite her legal woes. “Even the judges said she didn’t personally profit from the money, it was for her party,” he said of Le Pen’s newly upheld conviction for embezzlement . “All politicians in France have always bee

stories.title: Russia hits Ukraine's Kyiv, Odesa in fresh attacks
stories.summary: The strikes come with Ukraine running low on munitions needed to counter Russia's ballistic missiles. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy urged NATO members to deliver on promises made at the Ankara summit this week.

stories.title: Venezuela quake death toll passes 4,300 as scale of recovery effort looms large
stories.summary: Nearly 17,000 injured and thousands more listed as missing amid calls by president Delcy Rodríguez and UN for financial help The death toll in Venezuela’s devastating twin earthquakes has passed 4,300, the government said on Saturday. At least 4,333 people were killed and 16,740 injured in the back-to-back quakes on 24 June that flattened entire districts in the coastal state of La Guaira, the Venezuelan parliament chief, Jorge Rodríguez, wrote on Telegram. Thousands more people are listed as mi

stories.title: Wallabies’ pop-gun revival under Joe Schmidt blown apart as France unload heavy artillery | Daniel Gallan
stories.summary: Plucky defeats decorated with patches of excellence will not cut it for Australia with a home World Cup now looming large The camera found Joe Schmidt shortly after France had completed a 22-point swing. Australia’s coach had seen a 21-12 half-time lead obliterated in 16 brutal minutes. Schmidt, one of rugby’s sharpest minds, looked short of answers. The trouble was that the questions confronting him had obvious answers but almost impossible solutions. Why had Australia’s discipline deteriorated

stories.title: Indian tourists killed when boat capsizes in Vietnam
stories.summary: The Indian Embassy released the names of all 32 Indians who were aboard the boat that capsized near Phu Quoc island in Vietnam. At least 15 of them died.

stories.title: Typhoon Bavi hits China after battering Taiwan, Japan
stories.summary: Bavi has forced hundreds of thousands to evacuate in China, knocked out power in Japan and Taiwan, and triggered deadly landslides in the Philippines.

stories.title: Heat is especially hard on people taking antidepressants
stories.summary: High temperatures can increase the risk of dizziness, dehydration, exhaustion and other heat-related problems for people taking antidepressants.

stories.title: Spain wildfire: Firefighters begin to contain deadly blaze
stories.summary: Firefighters have started to rein in the wildfire that killed 12 people in the southern Andalusia region, which is home to one of Spain's largest expat communities.

stories.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz, US launches fresh strikes
stories.summary: The Iranian navy has said it closed the Strait of Hormuz after firing on a vessel transiting an "unapproved route." In response, the US launched a third set of strikes on Iran this week. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Two people killed and several wounded in mass shooting at Toronto festival
stories.summary: The shooting happened during a salsa event, Canada's largest Latin street festival. Toronto police say two gunmen were responsible but that the suspects have not been detained yet.

stories.title: Indian tourists among 15 killed as speedboat capsizes in Vietnam
stories.summary: Local media say 21 people were saved after the boat overturned in rough seas near an island in south Vietnam.

stories.title: South Africa World Cup midfielder Adams dies aged 25
stories.summary: South Africa and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams dies at the age of 25, just weeks after featuring for his country at the World Cup.

stories.title: US Democrat Ro Khanna says he was detained by armed Israeli settlers
stories.summary: Khanna, 49, was visiting the occupied West Bank when he was held for 90 minutes, he says.

stories.title: How a viral seal called Neil caused mayhem in Australia
stories.summary: The five-year-old elephant seal attracted global attention during his recent time ashore in southern Tasmania.

stories.title: Ice on testicles and donating blood - the myths sold to men trying to boost their sperm
stories.summary: With men’s fertility increasingly in the spotlight, we meet the men using unproven “hacks” promoted by influencers to up their sperm counts.

stories.title: How men with female surnames are standing up to ridicule in Kenya
stories.summary: Children often inherit their father's first name as a surname, but traditions are changing and causing upset.

stories.title: T. rex could become most expensive fossil ever - but it's a ​problem for scientists
stories.summary: A 67 million-year-old Tyrannosaurus rex will go on sale in New York with a pre-sale value of $30m.

stories.title: Muslim judge in India faces death threats after convicting 'cow vigilantes'
stories.summary: Tabassum Khan has been facing online abuse after convicting 14 Hindu men in a lynching case.

stories.title: Argentína kiszenvedte a hosszabbításban, Alvarez pazar góllal lőtte tovább a címvédőt az elődöntőbe
stories.summary: Argentína nem sok mindent csinált a mérkőzés nagy részében, a hosszabbítást viszont nagyon megnyomták, ott kettőt lőve eldöntötték a meccset. Svájc egy teljesen értelmetlen színészkedés miatt a 72. perctől emberhátrányban játszott. Argentína végül 3-1-re nyert, Angliával folytatják.

stories.title: Megakadt a labda a pókkamera kábelében az angolok első gólja előtt, a FIFA szerint erre semmi bizonyíték nincs
stories.summary: A norvég szövetségi kapitány szerint „Mindenki látta, mi történt”.

stories.title: Narine Abgarjan: Manyunya (részlet)
stories.summary: Az örmény Narine Abgarjan hatalmas sikerű első regénye még inkább önéletrajzi ihletésű, mint későbbi művei. Nem csupán az író gyerekkorának nosztalgikus emlékekből és gyermeki fantáziából újraalkotott világában játszódik, de az Abgarjan család ténylegesen is szereplője sok mókás jelenetnek. Narine és Manyunya kalandjaiban mindenki ráismerhet a barátság varázslatos élményére és arra a különös szabadságra, amit csak gyerekként élhetünk át. Olvasson bele!

stories.title: Kisebb földrengés volt Békéscsaba térségében
stories.summary: A 2,1-es erősségű rezgést az epicentrum közelében a lakosság is érezhette.

stories.title: A vb legértelmetlenebb kiállítását hozta össze a svájci Embolo egy színészkedéssel
stories.summary: Videóbírózás után kapta meg érte a második sárga lapját úgy, hogy a műeséssel nem sokat ért volna el. A kiállítás után zokogva hagyta el a pályát.

stories.title: Az érkező hidegfront miatt négy megyére adtak ki figyelmeztetést
stories.summary: Záporok, zivatarok, villámlás, szélerősödés és jégeső is előfordulhat.

stories.title: Az Egyesült Államok és Irán újra egymást támadta, Irán lezárja a Hormuzi-szorost
stories.summary: A feszültség ezúttal egy engedély nélküli útvonalon haladó konténerhajó miatt robbant ki. Válaszul az Egyesült Államok vasárnap 140 célpontot támadott meg Iránban, miközben Irán rakétákat és drónokat lőtt ki a szomszédaira, köztük az Egyesült Arab Emírségekre.

stories.title: Baleset miatt elmaradt Korda György és Balázs Klári koncertje az EFOTT-on
stories.summary: Háztartási baleset miatt azonnali orvosi ellátásra szorult az énekesnő.

stories.title: Tízezres tömegben tört ki lövöldözés egy kanadai latin fesztiválon, több a halott és a sebesült
stories.summary: A lövöldözés gyanúsítottjait körözi a rendőrség.

stories.title: Meghalt Lindsey Graham, Donald Trump egyik legfontosabb szövetségese
stories.summary: Az amerikai republikánus szenátor 71 éves volt.

stories.title: Argentin kapitány: A DNS-ünkben van a szenvedés
stories.summary: Lionel Scaloni szerint öt-hat jó passz is nehezen ment Svájc ellen, fejlődniük kell, de a csapat küzdőszelleme miatt nem aggódik.

stories.title: Hétfőn megsemmisítik az áprilisi választás szavazólapjait
stories.summary: Sok más választási irattal együtt, egyedül a jegyzőkönyveket őrzik meg.

stories.title: Új üzenettel frissült a Hirado.hu oldala
stories.summary: Lecserélték a korábbi, bocsánatkérő üzenetet, melyet az M1 adásainak leállításakor tettek közzé.

stories.title: Rábólintott az önkormányzat: 140 lakásos lakópark épül a Feneketlen-tóhoz
stories.summary: A XI. kerületi önkormányzat olyan döntést hozott, amivel gyakorlatilag eldőlt, hogy egy 140 lakásos, három tömbből álló lakópark épül arra a XI. kerületi, Villányi úti telekre, amelyen most az egykori MSZP-székház áll. A projekt ellen a környéken sokan tiltakoztak, a beruházó és az önkormányzat szerint azonban sikerült egy mindenkinek megfelelő kompromisszumban megegyezni.

stories.title: Egy 28 éves férfit gyanúsítanak Ann Widdecombe egykori brit miniszter meggyilkolásával
stories.summary: A rendőrség egyelőre nem talált politikai indíttatást az ügyben.

stories.title: Conor McGregor 69 másodperc alatt vesztette el a nagy visszatérő meccsét
stories.summary: Az ír ketrecharcos öt év után lépett újra ringbe, és rögtön térdsérülést szenvedett.

stories.title: Bellingham az élesen kritizáló kapitányának: „Talán fogalma sincs, milyen hasonló körülmények között játszani”
stories.summary: Az angolok két gólját szerző játékos szerint néha piszkosan játszva kell nyerni, szombaton, a norvégok ellen pont ezt tették.

stories.title: Műholdas térkép figyelmeztet, elég egy kis szikra és lángba borulhat Európa
stories.summary: A tartós szárazság és a rekordmagas hőmérséklet miatt nagy kiterjedésű erdőtüzek jöhetnek Franciaországban, az Egyesült Királyságban, és akár itthon is.

stories.title: Egy beteg hozzátartozóinak súlyos kifogásai miatt indult vizsgálat a szombathelyi Markusovszky Kórházban
stories.summary: Az eset miatt a Tisza Párt szombathelyi képviselője is teljes körű kivizsgálást követelt.

stories.title: MI-vel akarta kijátszani a NAV-ot egy vállalkozó
stories.summary: A bejelentett székhelyére cégtáblát és postaládát rajzoltatott a szerkesztőprogrammal, de az ellenőrök gyanút fogtak.

stories.title: Disneyland egy emlékezetes gyárlátogatásnak köszönhető
stories.summary: Hot dog illat, hullámvasutak és mosolygó, életnagyságú rajzfilmfigurák. A világ vezető élményparkjával kapcsolatban nem egy autóüzem az első asszociációnk, pedig az automatizálás rémálomszerű előretörése nélkül a Disney egyik legjövedelmezőbb üzletága aligha valósulhatott volna meg. Disneyland vattacukor-utópiává szelídítette a kor disztópikus rémálmait.

stories.title: 230 ezernél is több halott orosz katonát azonosított egy független orosz lap a háború 1600. napjáig
stories.summary: Közben újabb ukrán dróncsapás ért egy orosz olajfinomítót a Volga-vidéken, a támadásban egy gyerek is megsebesült.

stories.title: Hó a sapka alá: így védekezz a Mont Blanc-on a hőkupola ellen
stories.summary: Európa egyik legnépszerűbb túraútvonala, a Tour du Mont Blanc 170 kilométeren keresztül tekereg a Mont Blanc masszívum körül. 7-10 nap alatt végigjárható, és ezalatt gleccserek, nyáron is havas hágók, tengerszemek, mormoták és kőszáli kecskék várják a túrázókat. Második rész.

stories.title: Érkezik az újabb hőhullám Európába, még Skandináviát is elérheti
stories.summary: Két hónapon belül ez a harmadik tartós hőhullám Franciaországban, szenvednek a britek és felkészülnek a dánok.

stories.title: Geszti Péter: Elment a Peti. Elment a hangunk. Megszólalni sem tudunk nélküle
stories.summary: A teljes zenészközösség megrendülten búcsúzik az 56 évesen, súlyos betegség után vasárnap elhunyt Szolnoki Pétertől, a Bon-Bon énekesétől.

stories.title: Elcsukló hangon, könnyek közt, de büszkén értékelt a norvég kapitány
stories.summary: Ståle Solbakken szerint a nüanszok nem őket segítették.

stories.title: Három autó ütközött az M7-es autópályán, 5 kilométernél is nagyobb a torlódás
stories.summary: Bár a 30-as km-nél a korlátozás már megszűnt, a kialakult torlódás csak lassan oszlik.

stories.title: Közel 3 millió embert telepítettek ki Kínában az év legerősebb tájfunja miatt
stories.summary: A 144 km/órás szélsebességgel és heves esőzéssel érkező Bavi tájfun gyökerestől döntötte ki a fákat.

stories.title: FIFA-elnök: Vizsgáljuk a 64 csapatos vb-t, az a terv, hogy Trumppal adjuk át a trófeát
stories.summary: Gianni Infantino szerint minden nemzetnek meg kell engedni, hogy vb-részvételről álmodozhasson.

stories.title: Izzott a levegő, amikor beindították a kétezer lóerős Szergejt, és kormolt a Púpos is
stories.summary: Képek a több mint 140 éves Szeged-Rendező pályaudvarról. Itt áll az országban fennmaradt két MÁV 370-es gőzmozdony egyike, itt működik az ország egyik padló alatti vasúti kerékesztergája, és innen irányítják a szegedi nagyállomás forgalmát. Elmesélték azt, amikor óvodások miatt állt meg egy IC ott, ahol a menetrend szerint nem kellene.

stories.title: Protests engulf Indian state after rape and murder of 11-year-old girl
stories.summary: Innocent man lynched by mob in West Bengal as police killing of suspect further escalates tensions Protests have engulfed the Indian state of West Bengal after the rape and murder of an 11-year-old girl, the subsequent lynching of an innocent man and the police killing of one of the accused. Outrage erupted on Sunday after the body of a missing girl was recovered from a pond in a town just outside the state capital, Kolkata. Continue reading...

stories.title: Nigeria says army has killed 300 bandits in north-western state of Zamfara
stories.summary: Vigilantes also took part in the fight that raged all night and the following morning, residents say Nigerian soldiers killed more than 300 members of kidnapping and cattle bandit gangs in the north-western state of Zamfara this week, according to a government official. Government troops targeted the gangs in Gummi district in a two-day operation that “led to the elimination of more than 300 terrorists”, Zamfara’s information commissioner, Mahmud Muhammad Dantawasa, said in a statement. Continue

stories.title: Berlin hostage crisis: Woman freed after hourslong standoff
stories.summary: A man allegedly took a woman hostage in a supermarket, prompting a major police operation. The suspect reportedly "pulled out" a big knife before the supermarket closed on Friday evening.

stories.title: Georgia left off NATO summit partner list as critics decry isolation
stories.summary: The Georgian government is facing accusations of international isolation after not being invited to the NATO summit in Turkey, the first time a key partner has been excluded from the alliance's agenda.

stories.title: War in Ukraine: Kyiv hit by Russian ballistic missiles and drone swarm, 10 injured
stories.summary: Ukraine woke to fresh destruction on 11 July after Russia launched overnight strikes on Kyiv, leaving at least 10 people injured, including a child, according to Ukrainian officials.

stories.title: US and Iran trade escalating strikes as supreme leader is buried after days-long funeral
stories.summary: Renewed attacks the largest since an interim memorandum was signed this June, with Trump saying fragile truce is ‘over’ The US and Iran traded retaliatory strikes on Thursday as US president Donald Trump threatened to escalate the conflict unless Iran stopped attacking ships in the strait of Hormuz. Iran responded to the latest round of attacks by targeting US-allied Kuwait and Qatar and accused the US of striking near its sole nuclear power plant. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘The worst possible combination’: what has caused Spain’s deadly wildfires?
stories.summary: Blaze made worse by heat, strong wind and plentiful dry vegetation amid hollowing out of rural population Wildfire in southern Spain kills at least 12 amid heatwave As firefighters struggle to quell the flames ravaging southern Spain and doctors treat the injured, a horrific picture of the fallout is emerging. At least 12 people died in a fast-spreading inferno that ripped through Almería on Thursday, many trapped in cars as they sought to escape a blaze that scorched 3,800 hectares (9,390 acres

stories.title: Ryanair has axed its family seating policy – but kids’ fees still add up
stories.summary: The airfare for a baby on your lap could cost more than your own ticket. Here’s how airline charges and travel taxes can hit you Ryanair recently stopped making parents pay to sit next to their children but depending on the airline the hidden extra costs involved in flying with children can be substantial. In some cases, you can even end up spending more for the baby on your lap than you paid for your own flight. Your baby might not need a seat, but you are still likely to pay fees for them to t

stories.title: Panino police: packed lunch bans enrage Italians at pricey beach clubs
stories.summary: Child who ate smuggled-in homemade sandwich kicks off latest skirmish over private resorts As lunchtime approaches at Il Tirreno, a private beach club in Montalto di Castro on the Lazio coast, Beatrice Bordo, sitting in the shade of a blue umbrella, unwraps a slice of pizza. A pranzo al sacco , or packed lunch, has become the latest skirmish in Italy’s long-running beach disputes after a woman was confronted over the clandestine consumption of a homemade sandwich at a private establishment in Pu

stories.title: Alfred Dreyfus statue to finally receive permanent home in central Paris
stories.summary: Sculpture of Jewish army officer wrongly accused of treason has been moved around the city for decades For 40 years, the statue of Capt Alfred Dreyfus has been moved around Paris, never finding a permanent home. The French army twice refused to allow it to stand at l’École Militaire, where Dreyfus, a Jewish officer it had wrongly accused of treason in 1894, was stripped of his rank in one of the most notorious acts of antisemitism in France’s history. Continue reading...

stories.title: Irán új legfelsőbb vezetője bosszút esküdött apja gyilkosai ellen
stories.summary: Pár órával korábban Izrael arra figyelmeztette az amerikai kormányt, hogy merénylet készülhet Trump elnök ellen.

stories.title: Bellingham duplája döntött: Norvégia-Anglia 1-2
stories.summary: Anglia a hosszabbítás elején szerzett góllal győzte le Norvégiát, és jutott a legjobb négy közé a világbajnokságon.

stories.title: Új, szuperhosszú nyakú dinoszaurusz fajt találtak Thaiföldön
stories.summary: Egyetlen csontból jöttek rá thai paleontológusok, hogy egy új fajra bukkantak.

stories.title: Norvégia hatalmasat küzdött, de Bellingham becipelte az elődöntőbe az angolokat
stories.summary: Nagy meccset játszott egymással Norvégia és Anglia, az angolok pedig ugyan nem voltak kifejezetten jók, fordítani tudtak, 2-1-re győztek és életben tartották a világbajnoki álmaikat.

stories.title: Hosszabbítás döntött az elődöntőbe jutásról: Argentína–Svájc 3-1
When London stank to high heaven
stories.summary: In 1858, London sweltered through an unbearable heat wave. The River Thames reeked so badly it left people gasping for air. "The Great Stink" went down in history — and ultimately gave London a modern sewer system.

stories.title: UK police arrest murder suspect in Ann Widdecombe's death
stories.summary: Police in southwest England say they have arrested a 26-year-old man on suspicion of murdering politician Ann Widdecombe. Based on initial inquiries, they were not treating the case as terrorism or politically motivated.

stories.title: India news: US to seek Lawrence Bishnoi's extradition
stories.summary: US authorities have confirmed that they want to extradite Lawrence Bishnoi, who was charged with the killing of Sikh separatist leader Hardeep ​Singh Nijjar in Canada. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei vows revenge for father's killing
stories.summary: Iran's supreme leader said Iran "demands" retribution for his father's assassination in an airstrike. Meanwhile, Trump has threatened to "completely decimate" Iran should it attempt to assassinate him.

stories.title: Germany's richest man takes on Big Tech
stories.summary: Dieter Schwarz made his fortune from supermarkets, becoming Germany's wealthiest person. Now he wants to take on Google, Microsoft and Amazon and an entire region stands to benefit.

stories.title: China achieves first-ever controlled rocket booster recovery
stories.summary: China successfully recaptured the first stage of a rocket after a launch on Friday in a breakthrough for the country's space program, state media said. The first stage of a Long March-10B rocket separated from the second stage after liftoff and returned to a platform in the sea.

stories.title: Crews drain Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool again as part of troubled Trump revamp
stories.summary: Trump directed that the bottom be painted what he called “American flag blue” but problems began quickly after the initial work was finished.

stories.title: Watch: Inside the 30-hour dating retreat run by monks
stories.summary: Forget coffee or drinks, the latest dating trend in South Korea takes place somewhere very different, a Buddhist temple.

stories.title: The photo that shows who's really on top in Le Pen's party
stories.summary: Thirty-year-old Jordan Bardella had been groomed for success as France's next hard-right leader. He will now have to wait longer.

stories.title: Meet the crypto billionaires building a world where money buys you a vote
stories.summary: They think democracy has run its course - so they're setting up their own nations instead.

stories.title: Japanese school teaches children how to stay safe around wild bears
stories.summary: Children at a primary school in central Japan are taking part in a bear awareness programme, learning how to safely coexist with wild bears as sightings remain common in the area.

stories.title: Spain fans celebrate World Cup semi-final place after Belgium win
stories.summary: Spain celebrated a dramatic 2-1 victory over Belgium on 10 July as fans filled Madrid to cheer La Roja into the FIFA World Cup semi-finals.

stories.title: Newly-released video shows Peter Falconio's killer questioned
stories.summary: Australian police have released bodycam footage showing one of the last attempts to get Bradley Murdoch to reveal the location of Peter Falconio's body, on the 25th anniversary of his murder.

stories.title: Trump threatens to 'completely decimate' Iran, a day after claiming talks to take place
stories.summary: "1,000 missiles are locked and loaded and aimed at the Islamic Republic of Iran," Trump wrote on social media.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged theft of trade secrets - here's what to know
stories.summary: A spokesperson for OpenAI said in a statement: "We have no interest in other companies' trade secrets."

stories.title: 'We escaped Spanish wildfire, but our friends lost their lives'
stories.summary: Hundreds of firefighters in Spain have been battling a deadly wildfire in the south of the country.

stories.title: Furore in Nigeria over fake federal agency set up in government HQ
stories.summary: President orders investigation after fictitious body given funding, triggering renewed scrutiny of alleged corruption A fictitious federal entity that was allocated 1.3bn naira (£700,000) in Nigeria’s 2026 budget has precipitated a political storm in Africa’s largest democracy in the run-up to a general election set for January. The fake agency came to light last October when Femi Gbajabiamila, the president’s chief of staff, wrote to the police alleging that his signature, along with official s

stories.title: Mexico investigates whether US lied about role in capture of drug lord
stories.summary: Reporting suggests FBI involved in seizure of Ismael ‘El Mayo’ Zambada García from Mexican territory in 2024 Mexico has launched an investigation into whether the US lied about its involvement in the capture and secretive transfer of a top Sinaloa cartel member in 2024, in what would be a potential violation of the country’s sovereignty. The US has long denied it played any role in the operation to detain the drug lord Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada García, a founder of the Sinaloa cartel, inside Mexi

stories.title: Once again Trump brought his wrecking ball to the Nato summit, and once again the alliance survived. But for how long? | Paul Taylor
stories.summary: As the US president swung between threats to take Greenland and promises of help for Ukraine, pledges of a ‘stronger Nato’ were lost in the wind Nato leaders survived another nerve-racking summit with Donald Trump and the 77-year-old defence alliance lives to fight another day, proving its durability against Atlantic storms. But it will never feel safe as long as the unpredictable, vengeful and ruthlessly transactional US president is in the White House. As usual, Trump stole all the headlines a

stories.title: Violence on trains in Germany is on the rise
stories.summary: The verdict in the trial over the killing of a train conductor highlights growing aggression toward railway employees in Germany. What is causing it — and what can be done about it?

stories.title: Mosque replica and anti-immigration signs appear on Northern Ireland bonfire
stories.summary: A loyalist bonfire in Moygashel, Northern Ireland displays a mosque replica and anti-immigration signs ahead of Orange Order celebrations.

stories.title: South Florida's Palm Beach airport renamed President Donald J. Trump International
stories.summary: Since returning to the White House, Trump has taken repeated measures to put his name and image in official spaces, an abrupt break with American political tradition.

stories.title: IDF accused of ‘field execution’ of Palestinian driver bringing aid into Gaza
stories.summary: Local truckers’ association says it may suspend operations, after several eyewitnesses decried Ahmad Esleem’s murder A Palestinian driver bringing food aid from the World Central Kitchen (WCK) into Gaza has been killed by an Israeli soldier “in a field execution”, according to witnesses and the local truckers’ association, which said it may suspend operations in protest. Ahmad Esleem was shot in the head on Wednesday when an aid convoy stopped because of a breakdown to one truck soon after enter

stories.title: ‘A lot of red flags’: plans for New Zealand’s first datacentre spark concern as locals demand greater transparency
stories.summary: Plans to build a NZ$3.5bn datacentre in Makarewa in the country’s south has drawn concern about electricity and water use, and potential noise pollution People living near the site of New Zealand’s first planned AI datacentre are calling for more transparency about the project, especially about how the centre’s huge electricity and water use and potential noise pollution could affect them. Singapore-based company Datagrid has secured approval to build a NZ$3.5bn (US$2bn) AI datacentre on a 49-he

stories.title: Heatwave across western Europe – in pictures
stories.summary: Western Europe has been scorched by its hottest June on record, scientists have said, as the UK enters its third heatwave of the year and wildfires ravage France and Spain Western Europe records hottest-ever June as heatwaves intensify Continue reading...

stories.title: South Korea chip maker SK hynix rides AI boom raising $26.5bn in huge US listing
stories.summary: SK hynix, a supplier of advanced memory chips, has seen profits skyrocket thanks to the global race to build AI datacentres South Korean chip maker SK hynix set pricing for its mega US listing on Friday, aiming to raise $26.5bn as it takes advantage of the AI boom in what will be one of the world’s biggest ever stock sales. The Asian semiconductor giant plans to issue the equivalent of about 18m shares on Wall Street’s tech-heavy Nasdaq index later in the day. Continue reading...

stories.title: Developing countries spend more repaying foreign debt than on education, UN reveals
stories.summary: Unesco report shows children lost out to servicing debt in 113 countries, with 18 spending five times more on loans Most developing countries spent less on education than they did repaying debt last year, according to the UN, at the same time as global aid to education is predicted to decline by up to 30%. More was spent on servicing foreign debt than on education in 113 developing countries in 2025, according to research by the UN’s culture and education agency, Unesco. In sub-Saharan Africa, c

stories.title: Weather tracker: Typhoon leaves people stranded on rooftops in China
stories.summary: Eleven reported dead as flooding also brings danger of snakes, while buildings collapse in Mumbai amid heavy rain As the first typhoon to make landfall in China for the 2026 season, Maysak has caused devastating damage in southern and central regions. The Guangxi region received intense downpours of up to 280mm in 12 hours, causing rivers to swell and dam walls to break. By Monday morning, flooding across the city of Nanning and surrounding areas had resulted in many people being stranded on roo

stories.title: What happens when your fun-loving grandfather — a good husband, an upright citizen — turns out to have been a Nazi?
stories.summary: Since millions of NSDAP membership cards have been made available online, many Germans have been surprised to discover that their ancestors weren't always as innocent as family stories had led them to believe.

stories.title: California’s largest lake is turning to dust
stories.summary: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for people living near its shores. Authorities are working to stabilize the lakebed and lock down the dust. But for some families, it's too late.

stories.title: Vegan pregnancy tests and other eco wins this week
stories.summary: Keeping cool in the heat with a millennia-old tradition in India, a German company making animal-free medical tests, and building from bottles in Mozambique.

stories.title: Paris City Hall draped in Les Bleus colours ahead of World Cup quarter-final
stories.summary: Paris City Hall displays Les Bleus banners ahead of France's 2026 World Cup quarter-final against Morocco, showing support for the national team.

stories.title: Mud and debris left behind after deadly floods in southern China
stories.summary: Residents in Guangxi, southern China, clear mud and debris after deadly floods damaged homes and streets following heavy rainfall.

stories.title: At least 28 dead in fire at shoe factory in eastern China, state media reports
stories.summary: Footage shared by state television CCTV showed firefighters dousing the facade and interior of a large, multi-story white building with fire hoses, as thick black smoke poured out.

stories.title: Santas fill Danish streets for annual congress
stories.summary: Dozens of Santas and Mrs. Clauses gather in Aalborg, Denmark, for the World Santa Claus Congress, with parades, family activities and summer festivities.

stories.title: OPCW reinstates Syria’s voting rights, citing 'significant change in circumstances'
stories.summary: In 2013, Syria agreed to join the OPCW and disclose and hand over its toxic stockpile under Russian and US pressure and to avert the threat of air strikes by Washington and its allies.

stories.title: Iran buries Khamenei as renewed fighting tests shaky ceasefire deal
stories.summary: Khamenei's flag-covered coffin was carried aloft into the shrine of Imam Reza in his home city of Mashhad in eastern Iran as a sea of people waited outside and listened to prayers, with no sign of a public appearance by his son and successor Mojtaba Khamenei.

stories.title: Iran bids farewell to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei amid tensions with US
stories.summary: Large crowds of mourners marched through Mashhad on 9 July for the burial of former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at Mashhad’s Imam Reza Shrine.

stories.title: Pyongyang vows to strengthen nuclear force 'in quality and quantity'
stories.summary: North Korean leaders also laid out plans to accelerate the building of modern naval bases and to upgrade combat systems, per KCNA.

stories.title: Deadly fire at China shoe factory sparks Xi Jinping order for full probe
stories.summary: China is investigating a fire that killed 28 people at a shoe factory in Jinjiang, Fujian Province, on 9 July.

stories.title: Spain wildfire near Almeria kills 11 and sparks major evacuations
stories.summary: Spain is battling a deadly wildfire in Los Gallardos, near Almeria, where at least 11 people had died by 10 July after flames swept through a semi-arid area near the Sierra de los Filabres.

stories.title: Spain: Nine injured in fourth San Fermín bull run in Pamplona
stories.summary: The fourth San Fermin bull run was once again a fast and dangerous dash along the cobbled streets of Pamplona.

stories.title: Iran reports casualties and infrastructure damage after US strikes as tensions in the Gulf rise
stories.summary: Iran’s Foreign Ministry said the latest wave of US strikes constituted “a blatant war crime" and condemned what it called “aggressive actions” by the US military. Tehran responded with strikes of its own towards Gulf countries.

stories.title: A rock star welcome and human rights protests to greet India’s prime minister Narendra Modi in Australia
stories.summary: Amnesty International says Albanese has opportunity to ‘reaffirm mutual commitment’ to rule of law as thousands in diaspora expected at stadium event Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Indian prime minister Narendra Modi’s first visit to Australia in three years has sparked calls for the federal government to raise human rights concerns, as excitement builds within the nation’s Indian diaspora. Modi’s visit begi

stories.title: Marine Le Pen ‘wants to talk politics’, but can she drown out the legal noise?
stories.summary: The French far-right leader’s presidential campaign is clouded in uncertainty under the shadow of an embezzlement conviction Marine Le Pen ’s decision to run for French president in 2027, despite her legal woes, has drawn comparisons from her opponents to Donald Trump. Just as the US president felt his voter base cared little about legal investigations against him, the French far-right leader shrugged off the leftwing protesters who shouted “criminal!” as she launched her presidential campaign a

stories.title: Attacks on Kyiv ahead of NATO summit: What is Putin's goal?
stories.summary: Kyiv suffered heavy Russian strikes ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara. Experts say Russian President Vladimir Putin is trying to send a message.

stories.title: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has informed the Bundestag parliament of a deal to buy US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany. He said this closed a "important strategic ‌gap ​in our defense."

stories.title: EU rejects suspending biometric border controls despite 20 ‘difficult spots’
stories.summary: Officials admit new post-Brexit EES system is ‘not perfect’, as airports and airlines voice fears over delays The EU has rejected calls by airports and airlines to suspend the implementation of new fingerprinting and facial recognition border controls even though it admits there are “20 difficult spots” with queue chaos. With only a week to go before the peak summer holiday season starts, EU officials said the new entry/exit system (EES) was “not perfect” but it would tell travel industry repres

stories.title: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen says she will run for president in 2027 – video
stories.summary: Speaking in a prime-time interview on TF1 TV on Tuesday, the French far-right leader Marine Le Pen announced she will run for president in 2027 after an appeal court shortened her ban on holding public office. On Tuesday, the Paris appeals court upheld Le Pen's conviction for misusing European parliament funds but reduced the ban on running for office, clearing the way for the 57-year-old to stand in next year's election Continue reading...

stories.title: Populism unites Le Pen and Farage. But she is a step closer to power
stories.summary: Marine Le Pen is a convicted criminal. But now she’s running for office and there is still no credible candidate to oppose her As the judge read out the verdict in Marine Le Pen’s appeal trial for embezzlement, the same conversation was playing out in living rooms and WhatsApp groups across France. What? Does this mean she can run for the Élysée after all? But what about the prison sentence? And the electronic tag (which Le Pen had promised she would not campaign wearing)? And what about her pro

stories.title: Germany: Record debt and a seismic policy shift
stories.summary: The German government's draft budget for 2027 includes more money for the military and less for everything else, while debt continues to grow rapidly.

stories.title: US forces launch new strikes on Iran to curb threat to Hormuz, military says
stories.summary: Trump had said that the ceasefire with Iran is "over" after Washington launched strikes on the country overnight, to which Iran responded by targeting US bases in the Gulf.

stories.title: Dowry murders in India no longer spark public anger or debate, study finds
stories.summary: Thousands of women are killed in dowry disputes each year, despite the practice being banned in 1961 Dowry deaths in India no longer provoke the public anger they once did, despite thousands of women’s lives still being lost every year, according to new research. The killings – women who are murdered or driven to suicide following dowry disputes between families – have also faded from political debate, despite an increase in cases. Continue reading...

stories.title: Wildfires in southern Europe wreak havoc – in pictures
stories.summary: Wildfires raged across southern Europe, forcing thousands of people to evacuate their homes and prompting officials to ban spectators from a stage of the storied Tour de France cycling race Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Software glitch’ blamed for mass outage – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Telstra outage: Telco apologises for major time-keeping issue that hit mobiles, trains and triple-zero calls Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Telstra outage reported to be cause of Victoria train stoppage There is a long list of cancelled trains on the official V/line site, but the TL/DR is that Victoria’s regional train network has ground to a halt. Due to a radio network fault affecting the network, services are currently unable to operate …

stories.title: UK judges begin hearing appeal over Trinidad and Tobago anti-gay law
stories.summary: Activist is challenging ruling last year that restored colonial-era homophobic law against same-sex intimacy Some of the UK’s top judges are hearing arguments over whether a Trinidad and Tobago court had the legal right to overturn a 2018 ruling to remove colonial-era homophobic laws that criminalise anal sex between consenting men. The country’s “ buggery law ”, often referred to as its “sodomy” law, was created in 1925 and was written into Trinidad and Tobago’s 1986 Sexual Offences Act . In 20

stories.title: Pakistan locates wreckage of Boeing 737 cargo plane that went missing off coast
stories.summary: Early flight data shows K2 Airways plane crashed into sea with five crew on board south-west of Karachi Pakistan has located the wreckage of a Boeing cargo plane, the country’s airports authority said, adding that rescuers were searching for the five crew members on board when the aircraft went missing. The plane was approaching Karachi from Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates when radar showed it “rapidly descending” on Tuesday evening after reporting a “navigational system issue”, according to

stories.title: US charges Indian criminal gang leader with organising murder of Canadian Sikh activist
stories.summary: Lawrence Bishnoi, who is in prison in India, is accused of orchestrating assassination of Hardeep Singh Nijjar in 2023 US and Canadian authorities say they have “dismantled” the leadership of a notorious Indian criminal group, charging dozens of operatives who have “inflicted pain and cruelty on people, victims around the globe”, including a high-profile murder in Canada that strained diplomatic relations between Canada and India. At a press conference on Tuesday, members of the FBI and Canada’s

stories.title: Venomous snakes escape breeding farms in southern China during flooding
stories.summary: Local media in Hengzhou report king ratsnakes and cobras among hundreds in flood waters caused by typhoon Maysak Hundreds of snakes, including cobras, have escaped from flooded breeding farms in southern China as severe storms continue to batter parts of the country. State media reported that a snake farm in Hengzhou, in Guangxi Zhuang autonomous region, was hit by flood waters after days of heavy rainfall caused by typhoon Maysak , prompting warnings for nearby residents. Continue reading...

stories.title: LGBTQ+ cruise ship refused entry to Egypt days after Turkey turned it away
stories.summary: Scarlet Lady’s 2,000 passengers told of change as one of those onboard says they will ‘sparkle and spend elsewhere’ An LGBTQ+ cruise ship blocked from Turkish waters this week has been refused entry into Egypt. The Scarlet Lady’s 2,000 passengers, including the Broadway performer Patti LuPone , woke on Thursday morning to find a note placed under their cabin doors informing them that the ship was urgently looking for alternative ports. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine: Suspect in Monaco bomb attack found dead near Kyiv
stories.summary: Ukrainain prosecutors say the body of the woman suspected of the bombing that targeted businessman Vadym Yermolaiev was found near Kyiv. Police said they had detained two individuals suspected of murdering her.

stories.title: Heat-resistant housing: How we need to adapt our homes
stories.summary: In Germany, it is time to rethink and rebuild. Many apartments were not designed for heat waves. Fortunately, there are ways to adapt existing buildings to the changing climate — but air conditioning isn't the best.

stories.title: Conflict, climate change, cash-strapped: Why poverty persists
stories.summary: With just under five years left to complete the UN's Sustainable Development Goals, a new report shows what's hampering progress. And what the solutions are.
